note: Combined Sprint 5.37-5.39 Trinity External Worker Trial Pack + Queue Autopilot + Daily Report: three small bounded helpers landed on one branch trinity/external-worker-autopilot-report-v01. All three move Trinity closer to safe semi-autonomous operation without touching wallet/signing/broadcast surface. Pure scripts + schemas + data + docs + tests merge; zero src/, zero consensus, zero wallet/payment/broadcast changes. (A/5.37) New scripts/trinity/worker_trial_pack.py + schemas/trinity/worker_trial_pack_manifest.schema.json ($id=trinity-worker-trial-pack-manifest/v0.1). Emits portable read-only deterministic trial-pack directory a friendly second worker can run to prove cross-host replay equality. Pack contains PACK_MANIFEST.json (locked schema, per-file sha256), README_WORKER_TRIAL.md (run instructions for the recipient, contains the expected compute_output_sha256 by value), worker_config.json ($id=trinity-worker-trial-pack-config/v0.1 with preferred_backend=local_materials_engine_v01 + payout_address placeholder pattern ^<PAYOUT_ADDRESS_FOR_*>$ that REJECTS real sost1+40hex), sample_request.json (the deterministic request to run, copied from new check-in fixture tests/trinity/fixtures/useful_compute/request_materials_engine.json), expected_result_hashes.json ($id=trinity-worker-trial-pack-expected/v0.1 carrying canonical compute_output_sha256 + materials_engine summary + cache provenance). Six const-true safety_status flags at both script and schema level: no_wallet_required, no_private_key_required, no_seed_phrase_required, no_broadcast_capability, no_network_in_worker_process, pack_carries_no_secrets. Pack generator computes the expected hash via the operator-side worker pipeline (select_backend + run_backend over the materials_engine) so the recipient's worker MUST reach the same hash to pass the trial. Tests assert deterministic across reruns (byte-identical files + identical pack_id + identical expected_compute_output_sha256), no 64-hex blob unbound to a hash field, no real SOST address pattern, no /tmp absolute path leak, invalid worker_id/commit/tag rejected with rc=2. (B/5.38) New scripts/trinity/task_queue_autopilot.py + schemas/trinity/task_queue_autopilot_report.schema.json ($id=trinity-task-queue-autopilot-report/v0.1). Single CLI 'run-autopilot' that drives the existing Sprint 5.27 task_queue.run_batch + Sprint 5.28 task_queue_dashboard.build_dashboard up to --max-batches times (HARD CAP 24; --max-batches 25 refused at argv parse, returns rc=2). --max-items-per-batch capped at 50 (mirrors RUNNER_MAX_BATCH). Stops early when queue drained (stopped_reason=queue_empty), or with --stop-on-failure on first failed batch (stopped_reason=stop_on_failure), or after exhausting batch budget (stopped_reason=max_batches_reached), or on QueueError (stopped_reason=task_queue_error). Calls run_batch + build_dashboard IN-PROCESS — no subprocess, no shell, no network, no 'while True'. Writes JSON+HTML dashboard per batch under --dashboard-out-dir. Writes autopilot report at <queue-dir>/reports/_autopilot/TRINITY_TASK_QUEUE_AUTOPILOT_REPORT_<autopilot_id>.json with autopilot_id=tap-<16hex>, per-batch summary (batch_index, batch_id, attempted/completed/failed counts, safety_status), final_queue_counts (pending/running/completed/failed/total), every dashboard basename written + latest_dashboard_basename, stopped_reason enum, safety_status enum (ok/warning/failed), warnings array, and seven const-true safety_flags: no_wallet, no_private_key, no_signing, no_broadcast, no_autonomous_payment, no_network, local_dry_run_only. Tests assert max_batches > 24 refused, max_items_per_batch > 50 refused, max_batches = 0 refused, dashboard JSON+HTML files written per batch, report schema-validates, empty queue stops early with stopped_reason=queue_empty, autopilot does NOT import any sibling Trinity module other than task_queue + task_queue_dashboard (sibling import lint enforced statically), 'while True' rejected at lint time. (C/5.39) New scripts/trinity/trinity_daily_report.py + schemas/trinity/daily_report.schema.json ($id=trinity-daily-report/v0.1). Reads existing Sprint 5.28 dashboard JSON (+ optionally queue-dir for drafts/proposals), emits JSON + Markdown daily summary. Pure consumer of on-disk JSON: imports NO sibling Trinity module (enforced by static lint). Emits NO HTML, NO JS, NO inline CSS — Markdown only. Report contains report_id=tdr-<16hex>, counts (pending/running/completed/failed/batches), completed_items[] (per-item top_material + cache counts + workers_seen), failed_items[] (queue_item_id + watchdog_safety_status), top_materials[] (dedup sorted), cache_hits_total + cache_misses_total, workers_seen_total + worker_ids[] (truncated 32 chars), warnings[] (from dashboard), drafts_proposals_count + drafts_proposals_basenames[] (walks queue-dir for TRINITY_PAYMENT_DRAFT_*.json and TRINITY_PAYMENT_PROPOSAL_*.json), safety_status enum, safety_flags (six const-true: no_wallet, no_private_key, no_signing, no_broadcast, no_autonomous_payment, no_network). Markdown output has no <html>, no <script>, no <style>, no absolute /tmp/ path. Tests assert determinism, bad dashboard schema rejected with rc=2, missing dashboard rejected, sibling-import lint passes, html import lint passes (script must not import html), markdown structure asserts. Schemas all draft-07 with additionalProperties=false at every level, all const-locked safety flags, all $id values new. Tests: 1474 passed + 38 skipped on the deployment host (1367 prior + 107 new = 17 trial pack functional + 14 trial pack schema + 9 trial pack safety + 12 autopilot functional + 18 autopilot schema + 9 autopilot safety + 14 daily report functional + 14 daily report schema + 7 daily report safety). Manual demo over the Sprint 5.31 classifier-derived request fixture: autopilot --max-batches 1 --max-items-per-batch 2 processed 2 items, produced one dashboard JSON+HTML, autopilot report safety_status=ok stopped_reason=queue_empty items_completed=2 items_failed=0; daily report Markdown shows top_materials=[PrOx] cache_hits_total=4 workers_seen_total=4 worker_ids=[worker-A, worker-B]; trial pack for worker-D produced 5 files (PACK_MANIFEST, README, worker_config, sample_request, expected_result_hashes), expected_compute_output_sha256 baked in, all six safety flags const-true, NO real SOST address NO 64-hex private-key blob NO /tmp leak. Non-goals (documented): no live network call anywhere, no DFT claim, no wallet/key/seed/broadcast surface, no autonomous payment, no LLM, no new RPC, no new daemon, no new task_type/source_tool, no flipping manual_review_required, no daemonisation of the autopilot (one-shot bounded only).
